results.title: Polygram Competitors & Alternatives (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content: ## Design, code & ship products with AI. Polygram is building AI-native tools for software creation — helping teams plan, design, and build products faster using intelligent agent workflows. Our ecosystem currently includes: • AI-native vibe coding platform • AI-powered design canvas • AI coding agent for IDEs Unlike traditional AI coding tools, Polygram focuses on the complete product workflow — from planning and UI generation to production-ready development. Check out no-code and AI builders like bolt.new for prompt-to-app web stacks, Dreamflow and FlutterFlow for fast Flutter mobile builds, and WeWeb.io if you want visual control, hosted backends, and quick launches. HappycapyThe agent-native computer, for the rest of us. DreamflowThe Fastest Way to Build Mobile Apps, powered by Flutter. UI BakeryCustom internal tools on top of databases and APIs. YouWareWorld's first vibe coding community. WeWeb.ioThe best AI app builder for non-coders with big ideas. Builder.ioThe first AI agent for product, design, and code. LaunchCreate fully functional apps with AI and real human support.

results.title: what coding agent have you actually settled on? : r/ChatGPTCoding
results.content: # what coding agent have you actually settled on? Skip to main contentwhat coding agent have you actually settled on? Image 1 Go to ChatGPTCoding. i’ve tried most of the usual suspects like cursor, roo/cline, augment and a few others. right now i mostly stick to cursor as my IDE and use claude code when I need something heavier. Image 3: Clickable image which will reveal the video player: Try Copilot in the apps you know. (Thinking) or Gemini 3 Pro (high), for generating design documents that you then feed to a simpler model, is a great way to make good use of that power. I use what I guess Microsoft is now calling "Spec-driven development", when I code with AIs. I start with a specification and then work with the agent to hammer out a design document. I'll use Gemini 3 Pro (high) to generate the design and then switch to Claude Sonnet 4.5 to actually do the implementation.

results.title: I tested 9 Opensource Coding AI Agents so that you don't have to
results.content: I tested 9 Opensource Coding AI Agents so that you don't have to Here's a… | Rakesh Gohel | 52 comments 📌 Check out a comparison of Paid Coding Agents - https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rakeshgohel01_coding-agents-were-the-most-used-ai-agent-activity-7292187675921367040-CDBH?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop 📌 You don't need expensive LLMs to make your AI Agents better - Here's what I mean: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rakeshgohel01_you-can-build-better-reasoning-ai-agents-activity-7292912659585282050-uwzI?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop If you're into AI agents that can code, reason, and execute tasks autonomously, check out Quantalogic:https://github.com/quantalogic/quantalogic What makes it special: - Support code editing, efficient code search - Enterprise-ready with Docker-based secure code execution - Real-time monitoring with a slick web interface - Universal LLM support (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, etc.) ⭐️ If this sounds useful for your AI projects, a star would mean the world to me!
